title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Rhode Island
description: Rhode Island voters will elect one U.S. senator in 2026 for the state’s Class II seat. The race matters because it will help determine control of the U.S. Senate, and Rhode Island is widely rated as safely Democratic based on recent election history and current race ratings. The Democratic primary features incumbent Jack Reed and challenger Connor Burbridge, while the Republican primary includes Raymond McKay and Allen Waters; independent Jon Lindley is also running in the general election. The main contrasts so far are between Reed’s long incumbency and institutional record, Burbridge’s progressive insurgent campaign, and the Republican candidates’ efforts to break through in a heavily Democratic state.
polling_note: No public polling found for this race as of 2026-04-01.

candidates.name: Jack Reed
candidates.summary: Jack Reed is the incumbent U.S. senator from Rhode Island and has held the seat since 1997. His campaign and Senate biography emphasize his work on climate change, clean energy, Medicare and Social Security, and bipartisan legislating.

summary_sources.title: United States Senate election in Rhode Island, 2026 - Ballotpedia

career_history.title: U.S. Senator
career_history.description: Represents Rhode Island in the Senate; currently the senior senator from the state.

career_history.title: U.S. Representative
career_history.description: Represented Rhode Island in the House before election to the Senate.

career_history.title: Officer
career_history.description: Served as an Army officer after graduating from West Point; later taught at West Point.

education.institution: United States Military Academy at West Point

candidates.name: Connor Burbridge
candidates.summary: Connor Burbridge is challenging Reed in the Democratic primary as a progressive candidate. His campaign website says he is running against the Democratic establishment and is focused on Medicare for All, climate action, income inequality, education, and rebuilding the social safety net.

summary_sources.title: Burbridge for RI | Connor Burbridge Progressive Democratic Candidate for U.S. Senate Rhode Island
Connor Burbridge is running for Senate in Rhode Island ... - The Indy

career_history.title: Elder-care worker
career_history.description: Reportedly works in elder care and at a social services nonprofit providing housing and healthcare support.

career_history.title: Fundraising strategist
career_history.description: LinkedIn and campaign materials describe him as working in fundraising and advocacy-related roles.

candidates.name: Raymond McKay
candidates.summary: Raymond McKay is running in the Republican primary for Rhode Island’s U.S. Senate seat. Public descriptions of his campaign portray him as a longtime Rhode Island resident and veteran with a business background, and his prior Senate run makes him one of the more established Republican contenders in the race.

summary_sources.title: MEET RAY MCKAY - McKay for U.S. Senate
Where They Stand: US Senate candidate Ray McKay - WPRI.com

career_history.title: U.S. Senate candidate
career_history.description: Ran for U.S. Senate in 2024 and is again seeking the Republican nomination in 2026.

career_history.title: Business professional
career_history.description: Campaign biography and media coverage describe extensive business experience.

career_history.title: U.S. Army veteran
career_history.description: Campaign materials describe him as an Army veteran.

candidates.name: Allen Waters
candidates.summary: Allen Waters is also running in the Republican primary for the Rhode Island Senate seat. He has previously run for U.S. Senate and other offices, and his campaign branding presents him as a Providence-based, working-class candidate focused on conservative themes and local identity.

career_history.title: Candidate for U.S. House
career_history.description: Previously ran for Congress in Rhode Island.

candidates.name: Jon Lindley
candidates.summary: Jon Lindley is running as an independent in the general election for Rhode Island’s U.S. Senate seat. Ballotpedia lists him as an official candidate, but there is limited publicly available biographical information in the sources reviewed.
